description: Golden Benchmark Corpus for SUM Extraction Validation (Phase 19B)

documents.text: The Earth orbits the Sun at an average distance of 149.6 million kilometers.
Water consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen using sunlight.
DNA stores genetic information in all known living organisms.
Gravity is a fundamental force that attracts objects with mass toward each other.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.
Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
The human brain contains approximately 86 billion neurons.
Iron is the most abundant element in the Earth's core.
gold_triplets: most abundant element in earth core

documents.text: Dark matter may account for approximately 27% of the universe's mass-energy.
Some researchers suggest that gut microbiome might influence mental health outcomes.
It is possible that quantum entanglement could enable faster-than-light communication, though most physicists consider this unlikely.
Preliminary evidence indicates that intermittent fasting may reduce inflammation markers.
The protein could potentially serve as a biomarker for early Alzheimer's detection.
Scientists believe that approximately 95% of the ocean floor remains unexplored.
This compound appears to inhibit tumor growth in vitro, but clinical trials are needed.
There is growing consensus that sleep deprivation likely impairs cognitive function.
Mercury does not have any moons.
documents.notes: Negation should be flagged, not extracted as positive fact

documents.text: Vaccines do not cause autism. This has been confirmed by multiple large-scale studies.
The study found no significant correlation between phone usage and cancer rates.
Unlike popular belief, cracking knuckles does not lead to arthritis.
The experiment failed to demonstrate that the drug reduces blood pressure.
There is no evidence that homeopathy is more effective than placebo.
Goldfish do not have a three-second memory. They can remember things for months.
documents.notes: First sentence is negation, second is positive fact

documents.text: The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye.
The enzyme — which was first isolated in 1953 — catalyzes the hydrolysis of ATP; this process releases energy for cellular work.
According to Smith et al. (2019), "the compound exhibits remarkable selectivity" for dopamine receptors (D2 subtype).
Temperature (measured in °C) affects reaction rate: higher temps → faster reactions; lower temps → slower reactions.
The patient's symptoms included: (1) fever, (2) cough, and (3) shortness of breath — all consistent with pneumonia.
CO₂ + H₂O ⇌ H₂CO₃ (carbonic acid); this equilibrium is critical for blood pH regulation.
The protein (MW: ~67 kDa) binds to the receptor's extracellular domain; Kd = 2.3 nM.
Aspirin reduces fever. Acetylsalicylic acid lowers body temperature.
documents.notes: Same fact phrased differently — should collapse

documents.text: The heart pumps blood throughout the body. Blood is circulated by the heart to all organs.
documents.notes: Active vs passive voice, same fact

documents.text: Insulin regulates blood sugar levels. Blood glucose is controlled by insulin. The hormone insulin manages blood sugar concentration.
documents.notes: Three phrasings of the same fact

documents.text: Lions are carnivores. Lions eat meat. The diet of lions consists of animal flesh.
documents.notes: Three phrasings of the same classification

documents.text: The Sun is a star. Our Sun is classified as a G-type main-sequence star.
documents.notes: Second is more specific — both valid, not true duplicates

documents.text: Caffeine stimulates the central nervous system. Coffee's active ingredient excites neural pathways.
The study found that coffee increases heart attack risk. However, a larger meta-analysis concluded that moderate coffee consumption decreases cardiovascular risk.
Vitamin C cures the common cold. Clinical trials show Vitamin C does not cure colds but may slightly reduce symptom duration.
The Earth is 4.5 billion years old. Young Earth creationists claim the Earth is 6,000 years old.
Salt raises blood pressure. A 2023 study found that moderate salt intake has no significant effect on blood pressure in healthy adults.
Exercise is beneficial for depression. Overtraining can worsen depressive symptoms.
Red wine is good for heart health due to resveratrol. The WHO classifies alcohol as a Group 1 carcinogen.
Essential amino acids include: leucine, isoleucine, valine, lysine, methionine, phenylalanine, threonine, tryptophan, and histidine.
The noble gases are helium, neon, argon, krypton, xenon, and radon. They are characterized by full outer electron shells.
Three types of rock: igneous (formed from magma), sedimentary (layered deposits), and metamorphic (transformed by heat/pressure).
The four fundamental forces of nature are: gravitational force, electromagnetic force, strong nuclear force, and weak nuclear force.
RNA polymerase transcribes DNA into mRNA. Ribosomes then translate mRNA into proteins. tRNA carries amino acids to the ribosome.
Vitamins are classified as either fat-soluble (A, D, E, K) or water-soluble (B-complex, C).
